entry.title: Default TLS 1.2 in SAP API Management - How to check?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

As per the below SAP post, the TLSv 1.2 has automatically been enforced in SAP API Management. So, my understanding is , any request from SAP API Management to backend system would be TLSv 1.2 secured protocol. But I would like to know, from consumer of API perspective, is it also the the same (request from consumer of the API to the SAP API Management) that the request is secured with TLSv 1.2 protocol?. Is there a way to check the TLS version in SAP API Management?. Much appreciate for any response!.

Limits in API Management 

Thanks,

Azeem

entry.title: PFCG: indirect user assignments cannot be manually changed
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

 

When we try to remove user assignment from roles, we have encountered that "Indirect user assignments cannot be manually changed" and cannot proceed further.

Message number S#082.

Please help us to fix this issue.

 

Regards,

 

Basis Team


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Remove button in SU10 without using Screen Personas
entry.content:
Hi,
Our team wants to remove the button for editing single user in tcode SU01.
They say that the only way to achieve this is to install Screen Personas add-on.
However, we in the Basis team are wondering if another solution exist, to avoid having to install an new add-on just to remove 1 button in SU10.
See attachment to view the relevant button.
 
Do you know of another solution for this?
Thanks.
 



------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Upgrade: A Comprehensive Security Framework
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Upgrade: A Comprehensive Security Framework

Upgrading to SAP S/4HANA is a critical milestone for businesses seeking enhanced performance, modern features, and future-proof systems. However, amid all the planning, testing, and execution, security often takes a backseat — a risk no organization can afford.

This guide walks you through a step-by-step approach to upgrading SAP S/4HANA with security as a priority. Let’s dive into the technical roadmap to ensure your system remains robust and secure throughout the transition.

Step 1: Pre-Upgrade Assessment

1.1 Analyze the Current Security Setup

Before starting the upgrade, analyze your current SAP ECC system for potential security gaps:

  • Authorization Review: Extract and evaluate user roles and profiles using tools like SUIM or AGR_1251. Identify and eliminate inactive users and outdated roles.
  • Custom Code Security: Use tools like SAP Code Inspector (SCI) or ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) to detect vulnerabilities in custom code. This ensures compatibility with S/4HANA’s simplified data model.
  • Security Notes Compliance: Apply the latest SAP Security Notes via transaction SNOTE, and address any unresolved gaps to ensure compliance.

1.2 System Readiness Check

Run the SAP Readiness Check to uncover technical and security prerequisites, such as unsupported add-ons, custom objects, or authorization gaps. Verify compatibility with the latest SAP HANA database version.

1.3 Establish a Security Baseline

Establish a robust security baseline:

  • Compare current system parameters (rz10/rz11) with SAP's recommended values for S/4HANA.
  • Document system configurations, user roles, and access permissions as a reference for post-upgrade validation.

Step 2: Preparing for the Upgrade

2.1 Secure the System Landscape

  • Create a Sandbox Environment: Clone your production system into a sandbox for upgrade testing. Mask sensitive data to prevent unauthorized access.
  • Strengthen Network Security: Validate and restrict connections between your systems using firewalls, encryption, and secure network communication protocols.

2.2 Hardening Security Settings

  • System Parameters: Adjust parameters like login/fails_to_user_lock and login/password_expiration_time to enforce stricter authentication rules.
  • User Authentication: Implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for admin users and integrate Single Sign-On (SSO) through a trusted Identity Provider (IdP).

2.3 Role and Authorization Adjustments

The SU25 transaction is your go-to tool for upgrading roles and authorization objects: 

  • Synchronize existing roles with new S/4HANA transactions.
  • Identify obsolete roles or transactions and clean them up.
  • Adjust roles to include updated or newly introduced authorization objects, especially for Fiori apps.
  • Transport the Customer Table

Note:

The activities mentioned above align with the specific steps of the SU25 transaction during the SAP S/4HANA upgrade. Each step plays a crucial role in ensuring that the authorization framework is seamlessly transitioned to the upgraded system. Below is the mapping of these activities to the SU25 process:

  • Step 2A: Automatic Comparison with SU22 Data
    Compares the existing customer-specific authorization values with SAP-delivered SU22 defaults to align with the updated S/4HANA standards.
  • Step 2B: Modification Comparison with SU22 Data
    Identifies and evaluates customer-specific modifications to SAP-delivered proposals, allowing informed decisions on retaining, adapting, or discarding these changes.
  • Step 2C: Roles to Be Checked
    Highlights roles impacted by changes in transactions or authorization objects, requiring adjustments to ensure compatibility with the new system.
  • Step 2D: Search for Obsolete Applications
    Locates and flags roles or transactions linked to deprecated functionality in S/4HANA, ensuring the removal or replacement of these obsolete elements.
  • Step 3: Transport the Customer Tables
    Facilitates the migration of updated authorization objects, roles, and changes to test and production systems via standard transport mechanisms.

Step 3: Upgrade Execution

3.1 Perform Data Migration Securely

  • Encrypt system backups during data transfer using SAP Data Volume Management (DVM). Validate data integrity with checksum tools.
  • Use the Simplification Item Check to ensure custom developments and data structures align with S/4HANA.

3.2 Secure Downtime Activities

  • Enable audit logging (SM19) to monitor all activities during the upgrade.
  • Restrict system access to critical users using an access whitelist.
  • Isolate the system by temporarily disabling non-critical integrations.

3.3 Execute the Upgrade

Use SAP Software Update Manager (SUM):

  • Select security settings, such as database encryption for the HANA database.
  • Monitor SUM_Security logs to address issues with roles or security objects during the upgrade process.

Step 4: Post-Upgrade Validation

4.1 Validate Authorizations

  • Use SU53 to test for missing authorizations and resolve issues promptly.
  • Assign roles for newly introduced S/4HANA features, such as Fiori apps.

4.2 Review System Security Settings

  • Cross-check system parameters against your baseline, especially for authentication and encryption.
  • Re-enable critical background jobs (SM37) while ensuring they comply with security standards.

4.3 Patch and Secure the System

  • Update the SAP kernel and apply the latest Security Notes.
  • Perform vulnerability checks using the SAP EarlyWatch Alert and address any security concerns.

Step 5: Post-Upgrade Monitoring

5.1 Enable Continuous Monitoring

  • Leverage tools like SAP Solution Manager or SAP Enterprise Threat Detection to monitor security events in real time.
  • Configure alerts for unauthorized access or suspicious activity.

5.2 Conduct Periodic Security Audits

  • Regularly review user roles and authorizations using SUIM.
  • Use SAP Configuration Validation to ensure your system adheres to established security standards.

5.3 Educate and Train Users

  • Train users on security best practices and changes introduced by SAP S/4HANA.
  • Provide detailed documentation for administrators to manage upgraded security features effectively.

    Security

entry.title: Calling Secured iFlow from SAPUI5
entry.content: Can somebody explain how can I call the Integration Flow SECURED Endpoint from SAP UI5? To call it in Postman, I first fetch the acess_token with token endpoint then request the json resource with that token. How do I implement it with SAP UI5, so the token of the currently logged in users is user? Also how make the SAP UI5 get the token when user logins? I implemented this scenario 100 time in Angluar and React with custom backend scenarios. There is literally 0 documentation on how to do this in the SAP environment.

entry.title: Is it a good decision for someone without an IT background to learn SAP Security GRC?
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have 5.3 years of experience working in an MNC in a non-IT role (BPO), and I am now looking to shift my career towards IT, specifically in SAP Security GRC, for better growth opportunities. I am not interested in programming languages, and have found limited opportunities in my current field.

I would greatly appreciate insights from individuals who have transitioned into SAP Security GRC from non-IT backgrounds or from those currently working in this domain. Is it a good decision to pursue SAP Security GRC for long-term career growth?

Additionally, I would like to know:

1. What is the average salary package for someone with 3 to 3.5 years of experience in SAP Security GRC?
    Are there any current job openings for candidates with this level of experience? What is the demand for            SAP Security GRC professionals in the market?
2. Are there opportunities for freshers in this field?
3. Could you recommend any reputable training institutes in Hyderabad for SAP Security GRC with contacts?
4. Is certification required after completing the course, and if so, what is the cost?
5. Is any programming knowledge (like Java, Python, etc.) required to learn SAP Security GRC? Is it a "no            coding" field?
6. Is certification necessary to get job calls and Interviews? If so, what is the certification fee?

Looking forward to your valuable feedback.

Regards,
Sridhar.

entry.title: prevent transport owner from transporting to production
entry.content:
Hi all,

Our system version is ERP EHP8.

I have this request form client to disable the transport admin access from moving Transport Request to Production which owner is their own ID. At the same time, transport admin still can move transport to Production for others.

Is there a control that can be placed by identifying the user group to prevent certain user group from transporting their own transport to Production but can transport for other user group to Production?

 

Thanks.

Best regards,

Scott

entry.title: S4HANA Upgrade from 2021 to 2023 -
entry.content:
S4HANA Upgrade from 2021 to 2023 -

Please let me know the checklist for SAP Security consultant. 

Checklist means - What needs to be done from Security side as a part of assessment.

SAP Community UI SAP GUI for Windows SAP Fiori

entry.title: Security Test Rise
entry.content: What can I test in my RISE environment by hiring a Pentest for my entire virtual environment?

entry.title: S2S VPN requirement?
entry.content:
Hello Experts:

I have inserted two images here for my question. 

Our client is an existing SAP customer (installed base) on ECC. They are moving to RISE with SAP and are raising questions about the requirement for a S2S VPN connection - as shown here from "Customer Premise" in the upper left hand corner.

They want to know WHY it a S2S VPN is mandatory. They would prefer not to have to use one. 

This customer would prefer to explore THIS option.

The reason why they prefer this option is that they do not want to have remote users - i.e. those working from home or in their offices and already "in the cloud" - to have to activate a VPN before logging into their SAP environment on RISE. 

Why is a S2S VPN mandatory? 

Thank you in advance

Dan Magni

Daniel.Magni@Ameri100.com

entry.title: Enquiry on SQL Anywhere 17.0.10.6089: "SAP JRE" JRE180 Security Vulnerability
entry.content:
Hi, I'm currently using SAP Anywhere 17.0.10.6089 as the database for our application. SAP JRE located at `C:\Program Files\SQL Anywhere 17\bin64\jre180`. The installed JRE version is 8.0.31.

Did this version of SAP JRE (8.0.31) have any known security vulnerabilities? If so, I would appreciate guidance on how to obtain and apply the necessary updates or patches to mitigate the risks.

Thank you.

entry.title: SAC Data Leakage Prevention
entry.content:
Hello SAC Technology Community

In different scenarios, I've been facing the question of how can we prevent data leakage on SAC. How can this be monitored elegantly? And are there options to get alerted by unusual activities?

It would be great if you have any ideas.

entry.title: SAP BTP Mobile Services Native/MDK App Authorization Concept
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am currently working with SAP Mobile Services. I created Mobile Services instance on BTP. Established trust between these two platforms. Then I created native/MDK application that is utilizing Sample OData ESPM.

I kept all the default setting for the Security on the Mobile Cockpit for the application except for the enabling the anonymous access.

I created custom branded client and built it via Cloud Build. The app is installed to the android device. Nevertheless, when I install it, it is still asking for credentials to login to the Mobile Cockpit.

Is there or what is the way to distribute the application to people that will have access to neither BTP subaccount nor Mobile Cockpit.

I went through the documentation but is not really helpful for that topic.

Any feedback is really appreciated!

Br,
Aleks.

entry.title: Evaluate High privilege profile assignments in SAP ECC
entry.content:
Dear


I request your support since we need to validate if it is possible through a report to obtain the following users:


User who assigned the SAP_ALL and SAP_NEW profiles


User assigned the profiles SAP_ALL and SAP_NEW


It is possible to obtain a daily report and send it to the corresponding audit emails.


Best regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: confidential enterprise projects
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

My client runs ETO processes in their business and regularly contract with military organizations. This generates additional S&A requirements as in some cases, the project information along with any associated transaction data needs to be restricted to users that are actively working on the project or on a need to know basis. My first thought is to use a cost center, but when digging, I noticed that enterprise projects have an authorization group and a confidential project field in I_EnterpriseProject. Is there any info on how to access/utilize the fields to restrict the visibility of the data.

Thanks

Tom

entry.title: Enabling Auto Provisioning GRC AC Workflow
entry.content:
Hello,

I have 3 default roles all users should get, and they should not require approval to be provisioned. I would like to enable auto provisioning for roles without role owners, but then that opens up a risk of roles being assigned that are outside of our design (SAP standard delivered roles, etc). that have no role owner.

Is there any setup up where I can avoid this and still accomplish what I am looking to do.

entry.title: How to check when the last execution of a transaction took place
entry.content:
There is a need to know how to find the last execution of a specific transaction code in the system.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Security Vulnerability in SAP Crystal Reports for Eclipse (JAVA) SP31 - CVE-2024-21742
entry.content:
Hello

Regarding Crystal report for eclipse (java) - SP31;

Looks like there is vulnerability CVE-2024-21742 in file:

lib/xmlconnector.jar/lib/apache-mime4j-core-0.8.9.jar

version 0.8.10 and beyond does not have this vulnerability:
https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/org.apache.james/apache-mime4j-core

CVE details:
Improper input validation allows for header injection in MIME4J library when using MIME4J DOM for composing message. This can be exploited by an attacker to add unintended headers to MIME messages.

Can someone confirm if this is effected by this CVE, and if so can this be a hotfix or new service pack?

Thank you!

entry.title: Is there a way to compare multiple Roles Between Systems?
entry.content:
I am trying to find if the roles in our Development client are consistent with the Production client.

If I use the comparison tool found in SUIM (tcode: S_BCE_68001777) I can only select one role at a time. 

I tried to create an ECATT script by I cannot bypass the log in to production step required by the transaction

Any ideas?

entry.title: Mass maintenance of PFCG role menus
entry.content:
Hello all,

I want to delete transactions out of role menus via a mass maintenance. The known PFCGMASSVAL seems to be suitable only for authorization data, and not for entries in the role menu.

Does anyone have an idea if it is possible to do a mass maintenance of PFCG role menu entries?

 

Thanks in advance and best regards

Daniel
